hotels.name: Royal Mansour Casablanca

hotels.description_en: The Royal Mansour Casablanca, originally built in 1953 in the district designed by town planner Michel Ecochard, is an emblematic building of Casablanca. Besides its history, it marks the crossing point between art-modern and contemporary architecture, initiated by the pioneers, and the modern architecture of the second generation of architects in Morocco. Although the demolition of the building is inevitable, it was decided to rebuild the facade identically and to revive the specificities of the time: reintroduction of the loggias and the volume of the lobby as well as the restoration of the initial interior garden.
